description: Fix prayer card layout overlap across all 80+ layouts by making row-fixed shrinkable and adding JS-based vertical overflow detection that scales prayer sections when viewport is too short (landscape). This is a two-part fix: CSS makes sections shrinkable so flex can contain them, and JS detects remaining overflow and scales content down.
steps: 1. In app/src/main/assets/web/css/screen.css, line 83, change `.flex-layout > .row-fixed` from `flex: 0 0 auto` to `flex: 0 1 auto` (allow shrinking). Add `overflow: hidden` to clip content gracefully when shrunk. Keep min-height:0, position:relative, z-index:1.
2. Do NOT add height:100% to .flex-layout. Do NOT add max-height to .row-fixed. The .app-screen already provides the definite height constraint via position:fixed + inset:0 + height:var(--app-vh).

3. In app/src/main/assets/web/js/clock.js, add a new function `autoFitVerticalOverflow()` that: (a) finds the .app-screen element inside #layoutHost, (b) measures the total natural height of all direct flex children (header.row-fixed + main.row-fill + section.row-fixed), (c) if the combined row-fixed heights exceed 60% of the container height (meaning the row-fill main area would be less than 40%), applies CSS transform scale to the prayer section to bring it within bounds. This ensures content remains visible but fits within the viewport.

4. The autoFitVerticalOverflow function should: get the .app-screen's clientHeight, find all section.row-fixed elements, measure their natural scrollHeight. If the ratio of section height to total available height is too large, apply a scale transform (via CSS transform: scale(X)) to the section element. The scale factor should be calculated as: availableHeight / sectionNaturalHeight, clamped between 0.65 and 1.0 (never shrink below 65% to keep legibility).

5. Call autoFitVerticalOverflow() from: (a) after mountLayout completes, (b) inside the resize/orientationchange handler alongside syncViewportHeight, (c) after applyConfigToDom completes. Debounce the calls from resize events (similar to how autoFitPrayerCards is debounced at 120ms).

6. The autoFitVerticalOverflow function should reset any previously applied transform on the section before measuring (set transform to '' first to get natural dimensions), then re-apply if needed. Use transform-origin: center bottom so the section scales toward the bottom edge where it sits.

7. Add a corresponding landscape media query in screen.css that reduces padding on prayer sections in short viewports: `@media (max-height: 500px) { .flex-layout > .row-fixed { padding-top: 2px; padding-bottom: 2px; } }` - this provides an additional CSS-only safety net for very short viewports without using max-height.
acceptance_criteria: A new `autoFitVerticalOverflow()` function exists in clock.js that detects vertical overflow and applies scale transform to prayer sections
autoFitVerticalOverflow is called on mount, resize, orientationchange, and after config apply
A landscape/short-viewport media query reduces padding to help fit content
The prayer section transform-origin is 'center bottom' so it scales toward the bottom edge
verification: Read screen.css and verify .row-fixed has flex: 0 1 auto and overflow: hidden
Read screen.css and verify .flex-layout does NOT have height: 100%
Grep for max-height in screen.css to confirm none is applied to row-fixed
Read clock.js and verify autoFitVerticalOverflow function exists and is properly integrated
Verify the media query exists in screen.css for max-height: 500px
